candidate.summary: Exercise the paired benchmark protocol with the same clean commit for both conditions.
candidate.hypothesis: Both conditions should preserve every P0 renderer.print semantic sink.
candidate.limitation: This is a demonstration-only self-comparison and does not measure an optimization effect.

scopeRationale: One P0 renderer phase is sufficient to exercise scoped admission, adjacent pairing, all seven scenarios, cross-condition sinks, raw retention, and the 42-row record without a product candidate.

candidate.summary: Skip logical-comment subtree scans for non-logical SqlPrintToken values without changing formatter output.
candidate.hypothesis: renderer.print mean time should decrease while SQL, parameters, whitespace, comments, and line breaks remain identical.
candidate.limitation: The result applies only to renderer.print and the seven P0 scenarios; it does not establish whole-formatter or production-workload performance.

scopeRationale: The candidate changes only the logical-comment predicate ordering during SqlPrinter traversal after tokens are built; SqlPrintTokenParser, formatter construction and result assembly, AST parsing, collectors, condition optimization, corpus, options, semantic sinks, and benchmark source are unchanged.

candidate.summary: Exercise Stage 3 full-profile runner mechanics with the same clean pre-R1 commit for both conditions.
candidate.hypothesis: Both conditions should preserve all 42 P0 semantic sinks while producing no optimization-effect conclusion.
candidate.limitation: This demonstration validates runner mechanics only; it does not measure R1 or any optimization effect.

scopeRationale: All six P0 phases are required to validate Stage 3 full-profile execution, sampling, sink comparison, artifact retention, and the 42-row no-effect record.

candidate.summary: Collect formatter parameters during the existing token-building traversal instead of unconditionally walking the AST again.
candidate.hypothesis: Renderer token-build and formatter mean time should decrease while formatted SQL and parameter results remain identical.
candidate.limitation: The result applies only to renderer token-build, hot-format, cold-format, and the seven P0 scenarios; it does not establish whole-library or production-workload performance.

scopeRationale: The candidate changes only SqlPrintTokenParser parameter collection. Token-build and both format phases execute that parser; renderer.print consumes a prebuilt token, and the analyzer phases do not execute the formatter token parser.
